title: The Proverbs of Hendyng
summary: CME00091 is the checked-in copy of a University of Michigan encoding of printed pages 182-200 from Hermann Varnhagen's 1881 Anglia article on The Proverbs of Hendyng. The XML omits Varnhagen's introduction and three-witness stanza-concordance table on pages 180-181, then serializes two complete manuscript versions of one work: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1, ff. 476v-479v, and Bodleian Library MS Digby 86, ff. 140va-143ra. Varnhagen directly transcribed those codices and compared them with the Harley MS 2253 version through Wright and Halliwell's 1845 printing; his notes identify the comparison source as H(alliwell), so no direct Varnhagen-to-Harley consultation is claimed. DIMEV v1.0 records the encoded incipit family as record 2800 and treats the Harley version with its six-line prefixed opening separately as record 3383. Complete public facsimiles of Gg.1.1 and Digby 86, complete scans of Varnhagen and Wright-Halliwell, DIMEV v1.0, and a modern scholarly article are cached locally. Manuscript-image licenses are noncommercial even though the underlying medieval work and nineteenth-century editions are public domain in the United States.
review_notes: The two XML divisions are parallel complete witness versions of one intellectual work, not two separate works and not ninety-three independent works.
DIMEV record 2800 and the related prefixed Harley record 3383 remain distinct catalog identities; their relationship is recorded without silently merging them.
Both complete manuscript caches retain their whole physical codices. The work starts and ends on shared codex surfaces, and the exact folio and canvas ranges are mapped separately.
Varnhagen's Harley comparison is routed through Wright and Halliwell's printed text because the article evidences H(alliwell) readings, not physical consultation of Harley MS 2253.
Provider image licenses and current access conditions remain separate from the public-domain status of the medieval text and old print editions.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00091
entities.description: The checked-in XML artifact, with two DIV1 witness streams and no encoded copy of Varnhagen's introductory pages 180-181.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00091
alternate_labels: Zu Mittelenglischen gedichten XI. Zu den sprichwortern Hending's
entities.description: The University of Michigan digital text represented by the repository artifact. The stable item ID and official corpus work list identify Varnhagen's Anglia 4 article as its source.

entities.label: Hermann Varnhagen, Zu den Sprichwörtern Hending's (1881)
entities.description: Varnhagen's article publishes complete transcriptions of the Gg.1.1 and Digby 86 versions, preceded by a two-page introduction and stanza concordance with the Harley version. CME00091 encodes only the two transcriptions on printed pages 182-200.

bibliographic.citation: Hermann Varnhagen, 'Zu mittelenglischen Gedichten. XI. Zu den Sprichwörtern Hending's,' Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 4 (1881): 180-200.
bibliographic.title: Zu mittelenglischen Gedichten. XI. Zu den Sprichwörtern Hending's

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Anglia 4
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 648-page PDF scan of Anglia volume 4

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1
entities.description: The complete 46-stanza Cambridge version directly transcribed first by Varnhagen. The work occupies ff. 476v-479v within a large trilingual compendium; both boundary leaves also carry adjacent texts.

date_statements.label: CUDL production date
date_statements.value: first half of the fourteenth century, after 1307

physical_description.extent: ii + 633 + ii leaves; The Proverbs of Hendyng occupies ff. 476v-479v
notes: CUDL records several scribes and a broad English origin; it preserves competing West Midlands and possible Irish provenance hypotheses based on dialect. Those witness claims do not locate composition of the abstract poem.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library complete facsimile of MS Gg.1.1
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 1,281-canvas CUDL sequence cached at a requested 1600-pixel-width profile without upscaling narrow images

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 86
entities.description: The complete 47-stanza Oxford version directly transcribed second by Varnhagen. It occupies ff. 140va-143ra; on f. 143r the poem ends in column a while the Old French Les proverbes del vilain begins in column b.

notes: Digital Bodleian describes a trilingual miscellany of religious, medical, and secular prose and verse made in Worcestershire. The witness date and origin do not automatically date or locate composition of the poem.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of MS Digby 86
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 430-canvas full-color IIIF sequence cached at 1600 pixels wide

entities.label: Digital Bodleian 23-canvas selected-image object for MS Digby 86
entities.description: A complete cache of a separately published 23-canvas slide-derived selection. None of its selected surfaces overlaps The Proverbs of Hendyng at ff. 140v-143r.
entities.reproduction_format: Twenty-three selected canvases from 35mm slides and filmstrips; partial manuscript coverage and zero target-work overlap

entities.label: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, Reliquiae Antiquae, volume 1 (1845)
entities.description: The printed intermediary explicitly used by Varnhagen for H(alliwell) comparison readings. It prints the full prefixed Harley MS 2253 version on pp. 109-116 and only opening excerpts from Gg.1.1 on pp. 193-194 and Digby 86 on pp. 256-257.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, eds., Reliquiae Antiquae: Scraps from Ancient Manuscripts, Illustrating Chiefly Early English Literature and the English Language, vol. 1 (London: John Russell Smith, 1845).
bibliographic.title: Reliquiae Antiquae: Scraps from Ancient Manuscripts, Illustrating Chiefly Early English Literature and the English Language

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Reliquiae Antiquae, volume 1
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 350-page PDF scan with cached scan-data sidecar

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2253
entities.description: The collateral complete version printed by Wright and Halliwell and used indirectly by Varnhagen for comparison. DIMEV separates this version as record 3383 because it prefixes a six-line opening beginning 'Mon that wol of wysdam heren' before the Jesus-Christ stanza.

date_statements.label: British Library date for Part II

physical_description.extent: 1* + 142 folios plus unfoliated paper flyleaves; the Hendyng version occupies ff. 125ra-127ra
notes: The relevant second part was written by the Harley scribe and is associated by the British Library with the West Midlands and Ludlow. This does not prove the abstract work's composition place.

entities.label: Cambridge, St John's College, MS F.8 (James 145)
entities.description: A collateral fragmentary witness represented by seventeen pieces once used as binding pads and now bound at the back of MS F.8. DIMEV describes their hypothetical continued foliation as 137-138 and identifies portions corresponding to five stanzas of the Digby text.

physical_description.extent: Seventeen fragments associated with two hypothetical leaves, described as ff. 137-138 if foliation continued
notes: DIMEV says the fragments were formerly used as pads of the host book. LAEME regards the surviving language sample as too small to localize.

entities.label: DIMEV records 2800 and 3383
entities.description: The version 1.0 Digital Index of Middle English Verse records the encoded incipit family as record 2800 / IMEV-NIMEV 1669 and separately records the prefixed Harley version as record 3383 / IMEV-NIMEV 2078.
notes: Record 2800 lists Digby 86, Gg.1.1, and the St John's F.8 fragments as three witnesses and describes aabccb six-line stanzas.

Record 3383 lists Harley MS 2253 and two short related fragments; its descriptive cross-reference points to unrelated record 2834 even though record 2800 points correctly to 3383. The inconsistency is preserved as a data question.
The record split is catalog evidence about version identity, not proof that the encoded Gg and Digby streams are separate authored works.

entities.label: Cameron Louis, Authority in Middle English Proverb Literature (1998)
entities.description: A modern scholarly discussion of the Harley version's rhetorical construction of Hendyng as a wise authority figure and of the poem's stanza-plus-proverb form.

bibliographic.citation: Cameron Louis, 'Authority in Middle English Proverb Literature,' Florilegium 15 (1998): 85-123.

editorial_practices.description: The XML begins at the Gg.1.1 heading on printed p. 182 and excludes Varnhagen's historical introduction and L/C/O stanza-concordance table on pp. 180-181.
Varnhagen's introduction aligns stanza order across L, C, and O, while local notes cite H(alliwell) readings. The manifest keeps direct C/O transcription separate from printed-Harley comparison.

rights.component: Underlying anonymous medieval Proverbs of Hendyng text and witness readings
rights.basis: The poem and its manuscript witnesses are medieval and far predate U.S. copyright terms.
notes: This conclusion does not determine rights in modern photographs, interfaces, editions, encodings, or scholarship.

rights.component: 1881 transcriptions, introduction, concordance, notes, and editorial matter

notes: This is a jurisdiction-qualified provider statement. Reconfirm the exact item label when the interface is reachable without the challenge.

evidence.locator: Complete XML audit; BODY/DIV1[1-2], PB, MILESTONE, LG, L, EPIGRAPH, Q, P, BIBL, NOTE, and TEMPHEAD
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the repository artifact, two serialized witness streams, printed-page selection 182-200, folio and column milestones, 93 LG, 558 direct stanza lines, 50 epigraph L, 70 epigraph P, 94 EPIGRAPH, 608 total L, 14 NOTE, 19 PB, and the 2016 review statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Middle English Compendium, CME source-file list with URLs, entry CME00091.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00091 as Varnhagen's 'Zu Mittelenglischen gedichten XI. Zu den sprichwortern Hending's,' Anglia 4 (1881), and supplies the stable U-M item link.

evidence.citation: Hermann Varnhagen, 'Zu mittelenglischen Gedichten. XI. Zu den Sprichwörtern Hending's,' Anglia 4 (1881), 180-200, Internet Archive angliazeitschrif04tbuoft.
evidence.locator: Printed pp. 180-200 / PDF pp. 192-212; encoded text pp. 182-200 / PDF pp. 194-212
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies the volume and article citation, Varnhagen's three-manuscript account, L/C/O stanza table, complete-publication claim for C and O, manuscript loci, 46/47-stanza counts, H(alliwell) comparison readings, prior-edition citations, letterform policy, article boundaries, and the encoding's omission of pp. 180-181.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, 'Trilingual compendium of texts,' Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1, PID MS-GG-00001-00001.
evidence.locator: IIIF metadata and all 1,281 canvases; target canvases 960-966
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark, trilingual codex identity, first-half-fourteenth-century date after 1307, English origin, parchment, extent, multiple scribes, material and dimensions, competing West Midlands and possible Irish provenance hypotheses, complete image inventory, exact folio-canvas mapping, and CC BY-NC 3.0 downloadable-image attribution.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection of CUDL MS Gg.1.1 canvases 960 and 966 within the complete cached sequence.
evidence.summary: Verifies the French rubric and decorated opening on f. 476v, the ending and Quod Hending formulas on f. 479v, adjacent text on the opening surface, a new Latin item below the ending, decoration and rubrication, full-color character, and exact shared-surface boundaries.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Bodleian Library MS. Digby 86, object 2d6dfc9f-2ab9-46bf-9b32-e52f0e6eabb3.
evidence.locator: IIIF metadata and all 430 canvases; target canvases 288-293
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark, complete-object identity, 1272-1282 date, Worcestershire origin, trilingual miscellany, parchment support, holding institution, complete upper-cover-to-lower-cover sequence, exact folio-canvas mapping, and CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection of Digital Bodleian MS Digby 86 canvases 288 and 293 within the complete cached sequence.
evidence.summary: Verifies the red Hending þe hende rubric and opening on f. 140v, the conclusion on f. 143ra, the Old French Les proverbes del vilain beginning on the same final surface in column b, rubrication and decorated initials, two-column layout, and full-color image character.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Bodleian Library MS. Digby 86 selected slide object 10ff1c2f-acd3-4370-b276-a7ad739d2dae.
evidence.summary: Confirms a second, slide-derived 23-canvas object with CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution. Its labels cover selected folios from 48v-49r through 206v-207r and omit the Hendyng locus ff. 140v-143r, so it is partial manuscript coverage with zero work overlap.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, eds., Reliquiae Antiquae, vol. 1 (London: John Russell Smith, 1845), Internet Archive reliquiaeantiqua01wriguoft.
evidence.locator: Title page; Harley text pp. 109-116 / PDF pp. 123-130; Gg excerpt pp. 193-194 / PDF pp. 207-208; Digby excerpt pp. 256-257 / PDF pp. 270-271
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies the 1845 title page, full Harley transcription, opening Gg and Digby excerpts, their source labels and old foliation, and the exact printed intermediary identified in Varnhagen's H(alliwell) notes. The cached scan-data XML verifies page mapping.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 2800 and 3383; manuscript entries BodDig86, CULGg11, CamStJohn145, and BLHar2253; bibliography entries Varnhagen1881 and Wright1845.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml; data/Manuscripts.xml; data/MSSIndex.xml; data/Bibliography.xml; README.md; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Supports work title and incipits, IMEV numbers, aabccb six-line form, three record-2800 witnesses and exact loci, separate record-3383 identity for the prefixed version, Harley and St John's context, Varnhagen and Wright-Halliwell page references, linguistic cautions, and CC BY 4.0 release license. It also exposes the record-3383 cross-reference inconsistency retained as an open question.

evidence.citation: Susan E. Deskis, 'Proverbs of Hendyng,' in The Encyclopedia of Medieval Literature in Britain, ed. R. Rouse et al. (2017).
evidence.summary: Describes the work as a thirteenth-century wisdom poem in end-rhymed stanzas, normally pairing an elaborating stanza with a proverb and Quod Hending tag, suitable for monastic and lay adult audiences, and surviving in three manuscript versions. That synthesis is attributed and reconciled cautiously with DIMEV's finer record split and fragment census.

evidence.citation: Cameron Louis, 'Authority in Middle English Proverb Literature,' Florilegium 15 (1998): 85-123.
evidence.locator: Printed pp. 109-110; PDF pp. 25-26; first-page rights notice
evidence.summary: Supports the anonymous/uncertain historical identity behind the Hendyng authority figure, the rhetorical use of the wise persona, stanza-plus-proverb organization, Christian framing and practical advice, and the article's individual-use-only download notice. Louis discusses the Harley form and is not used to override DIMEV's directly verified aabccb pattern.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2253, record 040-002048084.
evidence.locator: Identity, Part II date and origin, material, extent, access, and Digitised Content fields
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark, parchment codex, extent, c. 1330-1340 Harley-scribe Part II, West Midlands/Ludlow association, trilingual contents, physical restrictions, and the current contradiction between a full-digital-coverage statement and an unavailable legacy image link.

evidence.citation: St John's College, Cambridge, Library and Special Collections access and reproduction information.
evidence.locator: Rare Books Reading Room, appointments, image supply, and reproduction permission
evidence.summary: Confirms supervised onsite access by appointment, the Special Collections contact route, and the ability to request private reading copies or publication images with permission. It is a general service route, not an exact F.8 catalog description.

evidence.citation: Exact public-site searches for St John's College MS F.8 (145), James 145, and Hendyng fragments.
evidence.locator: Queries F.8 (145), MS 145, James 145, and Hendyng
evidence.summary: No exact public catalog page or manuscript-image object was found on the current St John's public site on 2026-07-12; DIMEV remains the exact scholarly identity source and the College enquiry route remains open.
notes: Negative search evidence does not prove that no internal catalog, preservation image, or mediated reproduction exists.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse rights and permissions statement.
evidence.summary: The current collection presentation describes its materials as public domain and uses the No Copyright - United States rights statement. The exact CME00091 interface was challenged during automated access, so the item-level application is recorded with medium confidence.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: Works first published before the current statutory cutoff
evidence.summary: Supports the public-domain status in the United States of the 1845 and 1881 editions as of 2026.

evidence.citation: CME00091 ignored source-cache integrity and hard-link audit.
evidence.summary: Verified every manifest-listed cache path, recorded byte count and SHA-256, PDF signature and page count, both complete manuscript canvas inventories, selected-object inventory, ZIP integrity, representative image dimensions and color mode, and true hard-link identity between isolated worktree and main checkout.

agents.name: Digital Index of Middle English Verse

open_questions.question: How should the complete Gg.1.1, Digby 86, and prefixed Harley 2253 versions and the smaller fragments be related in a current critical model of The Proverbs of Hendyng?
next_steps: Consult the most recent critical edition and textual study, including the St John's fragments.
Retain DIMEV records 2800 and 3383 as distinct catalog identities while testing their stanza-level relationships.
Do not turn shared proverbial material or Varnhagen's concordance into unsupported direct-copying edges among surviving manuscripts.

open_questions.question: Does Hendyng represent a historical author, a legendary wise persona, or a name constructed from Middle English hende to authorize traditional material?
next_steps: Review current work on the Hendyng-Marcolf framing and authorship of the compilation.
Keep anonymous creation separate from the text's recurring Quod Hending attribution until historical identity is demonstrated.

open_questions.question: What are the current mounting, date, material sequence, exact recto-verso order, public image status, and complete host-object extent of the Hendyng fragments in St John's MS F.8 (145)?
next_steps: Ask St John's Special Collections for its current F.8 catalog record and a complete host-volume image inventory.
Request both sides of all seventeen fragments and verify DIMEV's hypothetical ff. 137-138 reconstruction.
Record item-specific reuse terms before retaining or publishing supplied images.

open_questions.question: When will the British Library's claimed full digital coverage of Harley MS 2253 again expose an auditable image inventory, and what terms will govern download and reuse?
next_steps: Recheck the exact catalog and Digitised Manuscripts route after service restoration.
If public systematic retrieval becomes authorized, cache the complete codex and map ff. 125ra-127ra separately.
Until then use Wright-Halliwell as Varnhagen's evidenced comparison intermediary and do not imply direct image inspection.

open_questions.question: Is DIMEV record 3383's reference to unrelated record 2834 a data error intended to point back to record 2800?
next_steps: Report the asymmetric cross-reference to DIMEV with exact release locators.
Preserve the verified 2800-to-3383 link and do not propagate 2834 as a Hendyng relation without correction.

open_questions.question: Where within medieval English-language culture was the work composed, as distinct from Digby 86's Worcestershire production and the competing West Midlands or Irish provenance hypotheses for Gg.1.1?
next_steps: Review authorial-language and rhyme evidence across all complete versions.
Keep manuscript production, scribal dialect, possible provenance, and abstract composition place distinct.

open_questions.question: What exact U-M keying, transformation, and publication history produced the pinned CME00091 XML artifact?
next_steps: Ask U-M for item-level production records and recheck the exact interface after the automated challenge is lifted.
Do not infer OCR, manual keying, or a release date from the 2016 repository review note alone.
